title: Will Goes A Courtin

jokes.text: God knows I wouldn't do it.
jokes.setup: Phil tells Vivian to kiss her sister for him
jokes.punchline: Dry admission he wouldn't want to kiss his sister-in-law
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character-appropriate dry delivery. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: I just don't think that black is the right color for this kind of heat.
jokes.setup: Hilary wearing all black because she's mourning Trevor's death
jokes.punchline: Phil's practical concern about color choice in hot weather
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from Phil's practical mindset clashing with Hilary's dramatic mourning.

jokes.text: Oh, don't worry, Daddy, this dress is surprisingly cool. It's like I'm wearing practically nothing.
jokes.setup: Hilary reassuring about the black dress being cool
jokes.punchline: Inadvertent admission of how revealing it is
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary obliviousness - sets up her father's reaction perfectly.

jokes.text: She's not my daughter.
jokes.setup: Hilary's revealing dress comment
jokes.punchline: Phil's immediate disavowal of paternity
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and economy. Phil's embarrassed father reaction is universal and perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: Yeah, man, it's so hot out there the Uh-Huh Girls just went: [implied gesture/sound]
jokes.setup: Discussion about extreme heat wave
jokes.punchline: Visual gag of enthusiastic backup singers being too hot to be enthusiastic
jokes.explanation: Creative visual comedy that's very Will Smith - pop culture reference with physical comedy twist.

jokes.text: You know, that girl has got the mind of Willard Scott.
jokes.setup: Hilary suggesting she mention the heat wave in her weather report
jokes.punchline: Comparing her obvious weather observations to famous weatherman
jokes.explanation: Dated reference but works as Phil's assessment of Hilary's obviousness.

jokes.text: Hey. Come to think of it, you got his body. Y'all should team up.
jokes.setup: Phil's Willard Scott comment
jokes.punchline: Will suggesting Phil has the weatherman's portly physique
jokes.explanation: Classic Will fat joke about Uncle Phil. Predictable but well-delivered callback.

jokes.text: Sorry, Uncle Phil has left the building. You're now talking to landlord Banks
jokes.setup: Will asking for air conditioner repair
jokes.punchline: Phil switching personas to avoid family obligations
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Phil's legalistic mindset. Sets up the episode's central conflict.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, was I talking out loud?
jokes.setup: Will muttering 'You'll get the rent when we get the air' and Phil asking 'What did you say?'
jokes.punchline: Will's feigned innocence about his obvious challenge
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom beat but delivered with Will's charm. Escalates the conflict.

jokes.text: Oh, don't worry about it, it's a double negative.
jokes.setup: Will asking what Phil means by 'no nothing'
jokes.punchline: Carlton's literal grammatical interpretation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - pedantic and missing the point entirely.

jokes.text: Dad, this is an outrage! Three dollars for orange juice?
jokes.setup: Phil presenting Carlton with a breakfast bill
jokes.punchline: Carlton's shock at the inflated price
jokes.explanation: Shows Phil's pettiness escalating. Sets up Carlton's eventual betrayal.

jokes.text: Oh, and I've taken the liberty of adding a 15-percent gratuity.
jokes.setup: Carlton's outrage at the breakfast bill
jokes.punchline: Phil making it even more expensive with a tip
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Phil's pettiness. The formality makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Carlton. Of course you know this means war!
jokes.setup: Phil's breakfast bill scheme
jokes.punchline: Will's dramatic declaration referencing Bugs Bunny
jokes.explanation: Classic cartoon reference that fits Will's pop culture sensibility.

jokes.text: It certainly does. And we're gonna win, right, Dad?
jokes.setup: Will declaring war on Phil
jokes.punchline: Carlton immediately switching sides to his father
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - eager to please authority and missing Will's solidarity attempt.

jokes.text: Girl, I got to tell you, that suit look like a piece of 'good God' wrapped up in some 'have mercy' with a side of: [gesture]
jokes.setup: Woman asking Will about switching to a pool party
jokes.punchline: Will's elaborate compliment using food metaphor structure
jokes.explanation: Classic Will Smith flirtation with creative language and rhythm. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Come on, let's get out of this heat before I pop something, you know.
jokes.setup: Flirtatious conversation about revealing swimsuit
jokes.punchline: Will's barely concealed sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Typical sitcom innuendo. Works for Will's character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Oh, sorry, that 'LA' stands for Louisiana. Our high will be 112.
jokes.setup: Hilary reporting LA will have a high of only 65
jokes.punchline: Revealing she confused Los Angeles with Louisiana
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment showing her incompetence as weather girl. Well-structured reveal.

jokes.text: Don't any of you touch me.
jokes.setup: Hilary finishing weather report surrounded by fans
jokes.punchline: Immediate dismissal of the 'ordinary people' she just praised
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary hypocrisy - fake public persona vs. real snobbery. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: I wouldn't call it disobedience, sir. It's more like outright defiance.
jokes.setup: Phil calling Will's pool party 'disobedience'
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey correcting with an even stronger term
jokes.explanation: Great Geoffrey moment - helpful but making things worse. Perfect British understatement style.

jokes.text: Dance puppets, dance!
jokes.setup: Phil looking down at the pool party from above
jokes.punchline: Phil's villainous glee at controlling the situation
jokes.explanation: Shows Phil's petty tyrannical side. The theatrical delivery makes it memorable.

jokes.text: That's either him or a rhino that got his horn chopped off.
jokes.setup: Carlton asking if Phil is coming
jokes.punchline: Will's description of Phil's angry approach
jokes.explanation: Vivid animal comparison that's both insulting and descriptive of Phil's mood.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, Howard!
jokes.setup: Phil approaching the pool party angrily
jokes.punchline: Party guest's shocked reaction to Phil's intimidating presence
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction comedy showing Phil's intimidating effect on strangers.

jokes.text: And I'm in heaven.
jokes.setup: Steffi saying her parents are in Aspen
jokes.punchline: Phil's inappropriate response to the underage girl
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable joke that hasn't aged well. More creepy than funny by modern standards.

jokes.text: Well, they will be if you come out in the morning, Uncle Phil.
jokes.setup: Phil demanding everyone be gone when he comes back out
jokes.punchline: Will implying Phil is so ugly he'll scare people away
jokes.explanation: Another Will fat/ugly joke about Phil. Predictable but well-timed defiance.

jokes.text: I pay rent here too, eventually.
jokes.setup: Will claiming 'this is my house'
jokes.punchline: Admission that he doesn't actually pay rent on time
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware humor that undercuts Will's own argument. The 'eventually' is perfect.

jokes.text: What, a sit-up?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey saying Phil did something he's never seen him do
jokes.punchline: Will assuming it's exercise-related
jokes.explanation: Another predictable Phil fat joke but economically delivered.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm afraid. Hold me, Will.
jokes.setup: Learning Phil didn't eat the leftover food (showing extreme anger)
jokes.punchline: Carlton's dramatic fear reaction
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton overreaction. The physical comedy of him clinging to Will sells it.

jokes.text: Oh, gosh, now you've gone and spoiled the surprise.
jokes.setup: Will sarcastically asking 'what's he gonna do, sue us?'
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry confirmation that Phil is actually suing them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey delivery and great twist that Will's sarcastic suggestion is actually happening.

jokes.text: Your Honor, I'm from West Philadelphia, born and raised. On the playground is where I spent most of my days.
jokes.setup: Will giving his opening statement in court
jokes.punchline: Reciting the show's theme song lyrics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor referencing the show's own theme song. Instantly recognizable and quotable.

jokes.text: Or a high-priced degree from Princeton... like my worthy adversary, Judge Philip Banks.
jokes.setup: Will claiming to lack fancy courtroom tricks
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's facing a judge who is also his uncle
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the courtroom conflict of interest. Builds on the David vs. Goliath theme.

jokes.text: And this man is making a mockery of a finely cut Italian suit.
jokes.setup: Phil accusing Will of mocking the legal system
jokes.punchline: Will countering that Phil is ruining his expensive suit with his weight
jokes.explanation: Yet another Phil weight joke but cleverly structured as a legal counter-argument.

jokes.text: You, dummy.
jokes.setup: Carlton asking who Will's first witness is
jokes.punchline: Will revealing Carlton has to testify against his own father
jokes.explanation: Great economy and perfect setup for Carlton's conflict. The 'dummy' is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: When we prepared this case, you didn't say I had to speak against my father.
jokes.setup: Carlton being called as a witness
jokes.punchline: Carlton's naive shock at having to oppose his father
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton innocence/naivety. Shows he didn't understand the plan.

jokes.text: smile at me
jokes.setup: Carlton reading Will's prepared statement praising himself
jokes.punchline: Will's stage direction written into the testimony
jokes.explanation: Clever visual gag showing Will's self-serving script. Carlton's literal reading sells it.

jokes.text: All right, I'm guilty! I'm guilty!
jokes.setup: Phil beginning to cross-examine Carlton
jokes.punchline: Carlton immediately cracking under minimal pressure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - complete inability to stand up to his father. Great comedic timing.

jokes.text: Welcome to my turf.
jokes.setup: Carlton confessing immediately
jokes.punchline: Phil's confident assertion of legal dominance
jokes.explanation: Shows Phil's professional confidence. Good callback to him switching personas earlier.

jokes.text: Objection!
jokes.setup: Hilary about to reveal intimate details about Trevor
jokes.punchline: Will's desperate attempt to stop embarrassing testimony
jokes.explanation: Standard courtroom comedy beat but well-timed to prevent awkward overshare.

jokes.text: No, no, no, this party is crazy, fly, dumb, deaf, doogy!
jokes.setup: Phil's flashback to the pool party
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated 90s slang showing Phil's biased memory
jokes.explanation: Great period-specific slang pile-up showing Phil's exaggerated perception of the party's rowdiness.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, son. Here, here's $100.
jokes.setup: In Phil's flashback, Will complaining about a popped duck
jokes.punchline: Phil's fantasy of himself as overly generous
jokes.explanation: Excellent unreliable narrator comedy - Phil remembering himself as ridiculously generous.

jokes.text: Now, why don't you take your Humpty Dumpty butt on back up to the main house
jokes.setup: Phil's version of Will's disrespectful behavior
jokes.punchline: Another fat insult with nursery rhyme reference
jokes.explanation: Creative fat joke using Humpty Dumpty. Shows Phil's biased memory of being insulted.

jokes.text: Right now I'm considering their 15-year-old daughter.
jokes.setup: Phil asking Will to consider the neighbors
jokes.punchline: Will's inappropriate comment about underage girl
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable joke that hasn't aged well. Part of Phil's biased flashback but still problematic.

jokes.text: To hell with my parents. Will taught me that.
jokes.setup: Phil asking if Steffi's parents know where she is
jokes.punchline: Steffi's rebellious response blaming Will's influence
jokes.explanation: Shows Phil's perception of Will as bad influence. Escalates the conflict in flashback.

jokes.text: You call me 'boy'? You better step to me like a man!
jokes.setup: Phil calling Will 'boy' in his flashback
jokes.punchline: Will's aggressive overreaction in Phil's memory
jokes.explanation: Shows Phil's memory of Will being unreasonably aggressive. Part of the dueling narratives theme.

jokes.text: Check it out, the night was so hot people was pulling up on camels, right?
jokes.setup: Will giving his version of events
jokes.punchline: Extreme exaggeration about the heat
jokes.explanation: Great Will-style exaggeration that's vivid and funny. Sets up his version being equally biased.

jokes.text: I've got some old T-shirts in there that I was gonna drop off for the homeless... but you're welcome to put one on to cover up.
jokes.setup: Woman in revealing swimsuit at party
jokes.punchline: Will's ridiculously polite and considerate response in his version
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contrast to reality showing Will's sanitized self-image. The homeless detail is perfect.

jokes.text: Oh, Gayle, bring out that book of hymns I was telling you about.
jokes.setup: Will's saintly version of his pool party
jokes.punchline: Will suggesting religious activities instead of partying
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist take on Will's self-image as wholesome. The hymns detail is inspired.

jokes.text: Safety first, my little non-swimmer.
jokes.setup: Carlton wanting to swim in Will's version
jokes.punchline: Will being protective and calling attention to Carlton's inability
jokes.explanation: Great callback to Carlton's swimming inability while showing Will's protective fantasy self.

jokes.text: He may be an unreasonable, pigheaded dictator... but he is your father and my uncle, and you will show him some respect.
jokes.setup: Carlton insulting Phil in Will's version
jokes.punchline: Will defending Phil while simultaneously insulting him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contradictory statement that insults while defending. Perfect comedy writing structure.

jokes.text: Do you know how humiliating it is to have a son who can't swim?
jokes.setup: Phil appearing at the pool party in Will's version
jokes.punchline: Phil's embarrassment about Carlton's swimming inability
jokes.explanation: Good callback that recontextualizes the running gag as Phil's embarrassment.

jokes.text: If I want your opinion, I'll beat it out of you.
jokes.setup: Will trying to speak in his version
jokes.punchline: Phil's extreme threat of violence
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's version of Phil as unreasonably violent. Dark humor that contrasts with reality.

jokes.text: Hit the bricks, you little tramp! Out of here!
jokes.setup: Will's version of Phil meeting Steffi
jokes.punchline: Phil being unnecessarily cruel to a child
jokes.explanation: Will's exaggerated version of Phil as villain. The over-the-top cruelty is the joke.

jokes.text: And tell your mother your father has a girlfriend!
jokes.setup: Phil throwing Steffi out in Will's version
jokes.punchline: Phil adding psychological warfare to his cruelty
jokes.explanation: Escalates Phil's villainous behavior to absurd levels. Dark humor about breaking up marriages.

jokes.text: I intend to cite the landmark case of Rhode Island v. Schechter Poultry.
jokes.setup: Will announcing an appeal with a legal case citation
jokes.punchline: The case being completely irrelevant to landlord law
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist legal comedy. The specificity of the irrelevant case makes it funnier.

jokes.text: That has to do with the right to hang dead chickens in a butcher-shop window.
jokes.setup: Will's legal citation
jokes.punchline: Phil explaining how completely irrelevant it is
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan explanation that makes Will's citation even more absurd.

jokes.text: Well, then, you see where I'm going with this.
jokes.setup: Phil explaining the irrelevant chicken case
jokes.punchline: Will pretending there's a logical connection
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist logic. Will's confidence in nonsensical legal strategy is the joke.

jokes.text: Yeah, never rent to relatives.
jokes.setup: Will asking if Phil learned anything from the ordeal
jokes.punchline: Phil learning the wrong lesson entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency - Phil missing the real lesson about family relationships.

jokes.text: I'm sorry.
jokes.setup: Judge asking Will to swear to tell the truth
jokes.punchline: Will finally apologizing instead of taking the oath
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ending that subverts the legal formality for emotional truth. Perfect resolution.

tmdb_overview: When Phillip sues Will and Carlton for unpaid rent, two different versions of the dispute are aired - neither entirely truthful.
